title: Munsar lake and shrines
tagline: Munsar lake and shrines — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: Munsar Lake (મુનસર તળાવ) is water body constructed by Minaldevi, mother of Jayasimha Siddharaja, of Chaulukya dynasty.
overview.description: Munsar Lake (મુનસર તળાવ) is water body constructed by Minaldevi, mother of Jayasimha Siddharaja, of Chaulukya dynasty. It was named as Mansavor but due to Indiscretion it's widely known as Munsar. This lake is situated at Viramgam, near Ahmedabad.

topPlaces.name: Viramgam
topPlaces.description: Viramgam is a town and former princely state in the Indian state of Gujarat. The town is located in the Ahmedabad district, which contains the city of Ahmedabad, the state's largest city.

topPlaces.name: Bhojva
topPlaces.description: Bhojva is a village in Viramgam taluka, Ahmedabad district, Gujarat, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Munsar lake and shrines?
faq.a: The best time to visit Munsar lake and shrines is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Munsar lake and shrines?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (61 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (58 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Munsar lake and shrines?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Munsar lake and shrines at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Munsar lake and shrines?
faq.a: Stays in Munsar lake and shrines start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Munsar lake and shrines?
faq.a: The top attractions are Viramgam, Bhojva.
